mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent, it signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, with the signal Sharpe ratio falling below 0.1 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in industrial production signal supply-side capacity utilization tightening, which compresses margins and triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow strong INDPRO growth in greater than 60 percent of cases during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of months following CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeding 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with PAYEMS growth, with a p-value greater than 0.1 over a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, signals consumer exhaustion and causes immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observed quarters during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid industrial production growth outpaces demand absorption, creating inventory gluts that force corporate earnings downgrades and negative returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns follow strong INDPRO growth in greater than 60 percent of post-shock recovery periods, invalidating the inventory glut mechanism.
